{"key_event":"BSAC amalgamates and thinly administers Northern Rhodesia","reasoning":"The BSAC colonial administration had formal authority, taxation, police posts, and some wartime mobilization capacity, but its reach was thin, uneven, and heavily dependent on chiefs and local intermediaries. African communities and chiefly structures retained some capacity for local resistance or evasion, but autonomous associations, press, unions, and mechanisms to hold the colonial state accountable were very weak.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":3.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":4.0}
